# npm Package Upgrade Migration Result

> **Executive Summary**\
> The `sovereign-hud` React TypeScript project was scanned for npm dependency upgrades. All installed packages were already at their latest supported versions — zero dependency groups required upgrading. The project install, build, and all 7 tests pass cleanly on branch `appmod/typescript-upgrade-20260416200403`.

## 1. Migration Improvements

The upgrade scan confirmed that `sovereign-hud`'s npm dependencies are fully up to date. No package versions were changed. The workflow validated that the existing dependency set is clean, buildable, and fully tested.

| Area | Before | After | Improvement |

| SDK/Framework/Dependencies | npm packages at latest versions | npm packages at latest versions | No outdated packages detected |
| Maintainability | Unverified package lock state | Verified clean install | `package-lock.json` regenerated and committed |

## 3. Recommended Next Steps

I. **Review the upgrade branch**: The branch `appmod/typescript-upgrade-20260416200403` contains the updated `package-lock.json`. Merge or close it as appropriate.

II. **Create Pull Request**: Submit the branch for code review if the `package-lock.json` update is desired in the main branch.

III. **Schedule periodic upgrades**: Since no packages needed upgrading today, schedule a regular re-scan (e.g., monthly) to stay ahead of dependency drift.

IV. **Save as Custom Skill**: To reuse this upgrade pattern across other projects, save as `My Skill` from the `Tasks` section in the sidebar.
